chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.254 of 2026 (Nazeer Ahmed vs. The State) For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 16.03.2026 Mr. Khan Muhammad Sarohi, advocate for applicant Mr. Mushraf Azhar, Special Prosecutor, ANF O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.25/2025, u/s 6, 93(e)14, 15 of CNS Act, 1997 (Amended Act, 2022), registered at Police Station ANF Gulshan-e-Iqbal, Karachi, by means of this application. 2. Applicant was arrested on spy information along with a truck, which, he was driving from Bismillah Market, Gate No.1, near new Sabzi Mandi, Superhighway, Karachi, and on his pointation from the truck 54 kilograms of charas in 45 packets concealed in secret cavities was recovered. Hence, he was booked in the present case. 3. Applicant’s counsel has pleaded for bail on the ground that there is delay in the registration of the FIR; applicant is first offender; and the compliance of section 103 Cr. PC has not been made. In support of his contentions, he has relied upon the case laws reported in 2024 SCMR 934, 2025 SCMR 721, and 2025 YLR 2764. 4. On the other hand, learned Special Prosecutor, ANF has opposed bail on the ground that a huge quantity of narcotics has been recovered from possession of the applicant. 5. In our tentative view, applicant is involved in a heinous offence of recovery of huge quantity of narcotics and is not entitled to bail. The grounds taken by the applicant’s counsel for bail as above cannot be appreciated at the bail stage and need to be tested at the trial.

In our tentative view, applicant is involved in a heinous offence of recovery of huge quantity of narcotics and is not entitled to bail. The grounds taken by the applicant’s counsel for bail as above cannot be appreciated at the bail stage and need to be tested at the trial. Prima facie, a huge quantity of narcotics recovered from him has been supported by the witnesses, who have apparently no enmity or ill-will against him. The fact that applicant has a clean slate, insofar as previous record is concerned, is not going to help him 2 be entitled to bail in an offence, which is considered to be an offence against the society. We, therefore, do not find the applicant entitled to grant of bail to applicant and accordingly dismiss this bail application. 6. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA.
